passages.title: Dragon Boat Festival Diary
paragraphs: Sunday, 22 June
The notice about the dragon boat race finally came out last Friday. I was excited because my father was on a race team!

In the morning, my dad woke up early. We went to the river later and waited for the race. Then the judge counted down and the race began! I ran and cheered for my dad. His boat was very fast. Finally, his team won the race! They all sang their team song together. They were so happy!
In the evening, our family made zongzi and had a big dinner for the festival, and for the winner, my dad! It was the best Dragon Boat Festival ever!

passages.title: Dragon Boat Race Notice
paragraphs: Love boats? Like exciting races? The Dragon Boat Festival is coming. Come and join us!
Time: 8:30 a.m. Sunday
Place: Xingfu River
Xingfu Community

passages.title: The story of Nian
paragraphs: Narrator: One night, an old man came to a village. He saw the local people running away.
Old man: Excuse me, why are you running? What happened?
Villager: The monster Nian will come! Last year, it ate many animals. We are all afraid of it.
Old man: Wait! I have a good idea! I can help you!
Villager: The monster is very strong. You should run away to the mountains with us!
Old man: No. I will stay alone in the village and face Nian.
Narrator: The old man stayed in the village. Then, at midnight, the monster came.
Nian: What is that red paper on the doors and windows? I hate the colour red! Why are the windows so bright? I hate bright lights!
Old man: Aha! He looks worried.
Narrator: Then the old man set off fireworks. Bang! Bang! Bang!
Nian: Oh no! I hate loud noises! No, no!
Narrator: Nian was very afraid and ran back to the sea. The next day, the villagers came back to the village.
Villager 1: I can't believe it! All our animals are safe!
Villager 2: How did you do it?
Old man: Last night, I pasted red paper on the doors and windows and I also lit many candles. Finally, I set off fireworks. He ran away!
Villagers: Hooray! We are safe!

Narrator: From then on, on New Year's Eve, people get together to celebrate the coming new year. They paste fu and Spring Festival couplets on their doors and set off fireworks. This is why the Spring Festival is also called guonian.
